title: ipomopsis aggregata subsp. candida
intro: Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. candida, also known as white skypilot, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States, characterized by its white or pale purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues and its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. candida include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory symptoms, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
